state.title: Check remaining needed words

reasoning_details.text:
All the words I need are available. Let me now draft the tier-4 story.

Requirements checklist:
1. 4 paragraphs, 3-5 sentences each (13-18 total)
2. Both support words: "performance" and "amazement" must appear naturally
3. Same character: Emma (capitalized)
4. Sequence markers: "First" opens paragraph 1, "After"/"After that" in paragraph 3, "Finally" in paragraph 4
5. At least one "If...then" or "If...will" conditional sentence
6. 1-2 dialogue lines with quotation marks
7. Pronouns allowed
8. Every content word on allowlist
9. Concrete and realistic, no fantasy
10. Build on tier-3 scenario: Emma the dancer, theater, performance

Let me draft:

Paragraph 1 (First...):
First, Emma arrives at the theater early to prepare for her performance. She checks her costume in the mirror and adjusts the fabric. Her hands feel steady because she practiced every day this week. "If I stay calm, then I will dance well tonight," she tells herself.

Paragraph 2:
Emma walks onto the empty stage to feel the space beneath her feet. The quiet auditorium helps her focus on each movement she will make. She runs through the hardest part of her routine one more time. The stage lights feel warm above her head.

Paragraph 3 (After...):
After the audience fills the seats, Emma waits behind the heavy curtain. She hears the music begin and takes a deep breath. Her friend Maria squeezes her hand and whispers, "You are ready for this." Emma steps into the spotlight with confidence.

Paragraph 4 (Finally...):
Finally, Emma moves across the stage with grace and power. Every spin and gesture tells the story she has been practicing for months. The crowd watches with amazement as she finishes her final pose. Emma takes a bow and hears the loud applause fill the room. She feels proud because her hard work created a beautiful performance.

Let me count sentences:
P1: 4 sentences ✓ (3-5)
P2: 4 sentences ✓ (3-5)
P3: 4 sentences ✓ (3-5)
P4: 5 sentences ✓ (3-5)
Total: 17 sentences ✓ (13-18)

Sequence markers: "First" in P1 ✓, "After" in P3 ✓, "Finally" in P4 ✓
Conditional: "If I stay calm, then I will dance well tonight" ✓
Dialogue: "If I stay calm, then I will dance well tonight," she tells herself. (1 dialogue)
And: "You are ready for this." (2nd dialogue) ✓

Support words: "performance" in P1, "performance" in P4 ✓
"amazement" in P4 ✓

input.content:
[user]tell me a story about a performer.
[Ninereeds]First, Emma arrives at the theater early to prepare for her performance. She checks her costume in the mirror and adjusts the fabric. Her hands feel steady because she practiced every day this week. "If I stay calm, then I will dance well tonight," Emma says.

Emma walks onto the empty stage to feel the space beneath her feet. The quiet auditorium helps her focus on each movement she will make. She runs through the hardest part of her routine one more time. The stage lights feel warm above her head.

After the audience fills the seats, Emma waits behind the heavy curtain. She hears the music begin and takes a deep breath. Her friend squeezes her hand and whispers, "You are ready for this." Emma steps into the spotlight with confidence.

Finally, Emma moves across the stage with grace and power. Every spin and gesture tells the story she has been practicing for months. The crowd watches with amazement as she finishes her final pose. Emma takes a bow and hears the loud applause fill the room. She feels proud because her hard work created a beautiful performance.

Everything else looks good. Let me write the final story.
